reason: Both answers correctly identify the core reposition: drop AI-powered PM framing, lead with resource forecasting as a category of one, target a different buyer, and demote PM features to context. Both give concrete actionable moves. B adds one genuinely useful point A lacks: a caveat to verify from usage data whether forecasting is truly the retention driver before committing to the reposition, plus explicit buyer naming and a quantified ROI example. A adds a pricing angle and a crisp litmus test, but these are more incremental. B's style is terser and less polished, but that is not a correctness issue. On substance B has a slight edge.
